description_long: This object is a vibrant spray of pink and purple orchid flowers attached to a short, dark brown stem. The flowers are arranged in a gentle arch, each bloom displaying five broad, rounded petals with a central lip that is slightly ruffled and deeper in color. The petals exhibit a gradient of hues from pale pink at the edges to a more saturated magenta and purple toward the center, with subtle white highlights. The stem is relatively short and sturdy, with no visible leaves attached. The overall structure is delicate and elegant, typical of Phalaenopsis orchids often seen in urban parks, gardens, or as decorative elements in public spaces. The surface of the petals appears smooth and slightly glossy, reflecting light softly.
description: A cluster of pink and purple orchid flowers with rounded petals and a short, dark stem. The blooms are arranged in an arch and display a smooth, glossy surface.
description_view_0: This view shows the full arch of the orchid spray, highlighting the overlapping petals and the rich pink coloration.
description_view_1: This view presents the flowers from the side, revealing the thickness of the stem and the profile of the blooms.
description_view_2: This view displays the front of the orchid cluster, with the petals' symmetry and central lips clearly visible.
description_view_3: This view shows the back and side of the flowers, emphasizing the layering and arrangement of the petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a bilateral and slightly whorled configuration, with overlapping and layered petals radiating from a central axis. Each flower displays a distinct symmetry typical of orchids, with a prominent central lip and two lateral petals flanking the upper and lower sepals.
